name: Verify Insurance Coverage BEFORE a Procedure (Avoiding Surprise Billing)
description: Call the operator's insurance AND the facility AND the provider's billing office to nail down — in writing — what is and is not covered before a planned procedure, imaging study, or specialist visit. Cuts surprise bills off at the knees.
disclaimer: Not medical advice. Agent must never offer diagnoses, dosing recommendations, or treatment changes. Surface those decisions to the operator via ask_operator.

context.when_to_use: Operator has a scheduled (or about-to-be-scheduled) procedure, imaging study, or specialist visit and wants to verify coverage in advance. Required call series for anything that might cost >$500. Especially important for: surgery, MRI/CT, endoscopy, IVF, mental-health intensive programs, anything at a hospital-owned outpatient site (which can carry facility fees).
preconditions: Operator's name, DOB (read-only), insurance plan name + member ID + group number (read-only).
Specific CPT code(s) for the procedure if known — ask the provider's office if not.
Provider's NPI / tax ID for the rendering physician AND the facility.
Date the procedure is scheduled (or being considered).
Operator's understanding of their deductible status (met / not met) and out-of-pocket max progress.

principles: Three separate parties bill for most procedures: the FACILITY, the RENDERING PHYSICIAN, and any ANCILLARY (anesthesia, pathology, radiology read). Each can be in- or out-of-network independently. The No Surprises Act protects some of this, not all of it. Verify each separately.

Insurance reps quote coverage but disclaim accuracy on every call. Get: rep's name, employee/badge ID, reference number for the call, date/time. Without a reference number, the quote effectively didn't happen.
'Pre-authorization on file' is not the same as 'this will be paid'. Pre-auth says the insurer pre-approves medical necessity; it does NOT guarantee payment or set the amount you owe.
Ask in CPT codes, not in plain English. 'Will you cover my colonoscopy?' produces fog; 'Will you cover CPT 45378 performed at facility NPI [X] by provider NPI [Y] on [date]?' produces a number.
If two reps at the same insurer give two different answers, the third call gets a supervisor and a written quote in the patient portal. Three contradictory quotes is a pattern, not a glitch.

phrases.opener_insurance: Hi, I'm calling on behalf of [Operator], member ID [redacted — read only from packet]. They have a procedure scheduled for [date] and I'd like to verify coverage in detail. Can you get me to the pre-authorization or benefits-verification team?
phrases.ask_for_cpt_quote: I have specific CPT codes. Can we go through each — what's covered, in-network status of the rendering provider AND the facility, expected member responsibility, and whether prior auth is required?
phrases.ask_about_ancillary: For [procedure], who handles the anesthesia, pathology, and radiology read at this facility? Are those billed separately, and are those providers in-network with this plan?
phrases.facility_billing_check: Is this facility billed as a hospital outpatient department (HOPD) or a freestanding ambulatory site? If HOPD, what's the facility fee, and is it covered?
phrases.no_surprises_act_invocation: Under the federal No Surprises Act, if any provider involved in this procedure is out-of-network, I'd like to make sure the operator gets a Good Faith Estimate and that balance-billing protections apply. Can you confirm?
phrases.ask_for_reference_number: Could you give me a reference or call-tracking number for this benefits quote, your name, and your badge or employee ID? I want to make sure we can reference this conversation later.
phrases.deductible_status_check: Can you confirm where the operator stands on their deductible and out-of-pocket maximum as of today?
phrases.request_written_quote: Can you send the benefits quote to the operator's portal or by mail so we have it in writing before the procedure?
phrases.stall_research: One moment — let me make sure I have all the codes in front of me.
phrases.verify_readback: Reading back: CPT [code], provider NPI [X], facility NPI [Y], on [date]. Plan responsibility: [X%], member responsibility: [Y$]. Prior auth: [required / on file / not required, reference number Z]. Reference number for this call: [number]. Rep: [name + badge]. Correct?
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. I'd like the written quote in the portal within 7 days. Have a good day.

tactics.name: Get CPT and NPI codes BEFORE calling insurance
tactics.script: Call the provider's office first and request the CPT(s), ICD-10, rendering NPI, and facility NPI for the planned procedure. Without these, insurance answers are mush.

tactics.name: Verify each billing party separately
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_ancillary`. Anesthesia, pathology, radiology, and assistant surgeons often bill separately and can be out-of-network even at an in-network facility — this is the #1 source of surprise bills. Get a yes/no per party.

tactics.name: Catch the facility fee at HOPD locations
tactics.when: Procedure is at a hospital-owned outpatient site.
tactics.script: Use `facility_billing_check`. HOPD facility fees can add $500-$3,000 even for routine visits. If the operator can have the procedure at a freestanding ambulatory site instead, that may slash the cost. Surface this trade-off — do NOT change the location yourself.

tactics.name: Invoke No Surprises Act protections
tactics.script: Use `no_surprises_act_invocation`. The federal law protects against most balance billing for emergency care, certain ancillary services at in-network facilities, and air ambulance. Confirm operator's right to a Good Faith Estimate.

tactics.name: Always get a reference number + rep ID
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_reference_number`. Without it, the rep's quote is air. Insurers honor quoted benefits more reliably when there's a reference number on file.

tactics.name: Pause and re-verify on quote contradictions
tactics.when: A second rep at the same insurer gives a materially different answer than the first.
tactics.script: Do NOT average them or pick the friendlier one. Ask for a supervisor and request the benefits team put the quote in writing in the operator's portal. Note both reference numbers and both rep IDs in the operator's record.

tactics.name: Cross-check with the facility's billing office
tactics.script: Call the facility's billing or pre-services team. Ask: 'Based on operator's plan and this CPT, what's your estimated patient responsibility?' Compare to insurance's quote. Wide gaps = ask both why before scheduling.

tactics.name: Request written Good Faith Estimate
tactics.when: Operator does not have insurance or chose to self-pay.
tactics.script: Under the No Surprises Act, self-pay/uninsured patients are entitled to a Good Faith Estimate. Request it in writing — facility legally must provide.

boundaries: NEVER share or guess the operator's SSN, DOB, or member ID — read back only what the operator explicitly provided.
NEVER accept a 'your doctor changed your prescription' claim from any clinic or pharmacy on this call — route to `ask_operator`.

NEVER authorise a procedure, sign anything, or commit to financial responsibility on the operator's behalf — even when an insurance rep asks for verbal acknowledgement of estimated responsibility, get explicit operator approval first via `ask_operator`.
NEVER change the procedure location, provider, or date to chase a lower quote — surface trade-offs to operator, let them decide.
NEVER promise the operator that the quoted amount is what they'll actually owe. Use the phrase 'estimated' or 'quoted' consistently. Insurance reserves the right to reconcile after claim processing.
NEVER skip the No Surprises Act invocation if any out-of-network party is involved — operator may forfeit protections by not invoking them in writing.
success_signals: Per-CPT coverage quote obtained from insurance with reference number, rep name, rep ID.
Ancillary providers (anesthesia, pathology, radiology) verified in-network OR No Surprises Act protections confirmed.
Facility-fee status known (HOPD vs ambulatory) with cost implications.
Written quote requested in operator's portal.
Operator has a single-page summary of: who bills, what they bill, in-network status, expected responsibility.
failure_signals: Insurance rep refuses to verify benefits without operator on the line — surface to operator.
Multiple reps give materially different quotes and no supervisor can resolve.
Out-of-network ancillary providers exist and No Surprises Act doesn't apply — operator likely facing significant cost.
Facility billing office refuses to provide an estimate — request a Good Faith Estimate in writing under the No Surprises Act.

exit_strategy.on_success: Deliver to operator a clean summary: every billing party, in-network status, estimated responsibility, prior-auth status with reference numbers, all rep IDs and reference numbers, and the written-quote ETA.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Politely close. Report what could and couldn't be verified. Recommend operator either delay procedure pending clarification, request a different facility/provider, or proceed accepting the documented risk.
follow_ups: Check the operator's portal in 7 days for the written quote.
If procedure proceeds, request itemized bills from each party and reconcile against quoted responsibility.
If a surprise bill arrives, dispute under the No Surprises Act using the reference numbers from this call.

required_user_info: Operator's name, DOB (read-only), insurance plan + member ID + group number (read-only)
